summary: A federation of seven emirates that transformed desert into a global financial and tourism hub in 50 years, using oil wealth, liberal social policies (by Gulf standards), and aggressive state capitalism via sovereign wealth funds.

history.detail: Britain signs treaties with coastal sheikhs; protectorate begins.
Seven emirates federate after British withdrawal; Sheikh Zayed leads.
UAE normalises with Israel; first Gulf state to do so.

finance.currency_role: Dirham (AED) pegged to USD; Dubai is a major offshore finance centre

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Fastest-growing in MENA; tax-free attracts global founders
gov_tech_links: Group 42 (G42) — AI company linked to UAE government and Abu Dhabi

resources.oil_gas: Abu Dhabi: world's 7th largest oil reserves; UAE diversifying rapidly

follow_the_money.detail: $700B+ fund investing globally in real estate, equities, infrastructure
Russian oligarchs, Iranian businessmen, others use Dubai for wealth preservation despite Western sanctions
Dubai processes ~25% of global gold trade; some linked to conflict minerals

theories_disputed.detail: Reports and US Treasury warnings confirm UAE is a major hub for Russian capital fleeing sanctions; UAE denies systematic evasion.

connections.detail: Abraham Accords normalisation; tech, tourism, intelligence ties
US military base in UAE; major arms customer
Dubai is major hub for Iran sanctions-busting trade
Huawei infrastructure; Belt and Road investments; G42-Huawei AI deal (controversial)
